Records.authors: Gambuti, Giulio, Kosower, David A., Novichkov, Pavel P., Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Massless three-loop planar ladder integral, the $L=3$ instance of the all-loop ladder conjecture. The conjecture gives 2 rank-2, 6 rank-3, and 9 rank-4 locally finite generators (17 total) with no generators beyond rank 4. Evanescent generators appear at ranks 4 through 6. The conjecture is verified by explicit computation at $L=3$ and $L=4$.

Massless three-loop planar ladder-box (triple-box) with all external legs massless. 26 master integrals in canonical basis, solutions in GPLs of $y=s_{23}/s_{12}$ with 2-letter alphabet $\{0,1\}$. Obtained via $x \to 1$ resummation of the one-mass SDE family.

Three-loop massless on-shell planar triple box Feynman integral $T(1,\ldots,1;s,t;\varepsilon)$ with all ten propagator powers equal to one and on-shell external legs $p_i^2=0$, evaluated analytically for general Mandelstam $s,t$ through the finite part. Full Laurent expansion from the leading $1/\varepsilon^6$ pole through $\varepsilon^0$ in Eq. (3box-result), expressed in harmonic polylogarithms $H_{a_1,\ldots,a_n}(x)$ of weight up to six with indices in $\{0,1\}$, $x=-t/s$, $L=\ln(s/t)$. Evaluation uses alpha (Feynman) parameters together with a sevenfold Mellin-Barnes representation, then residues/contour shifts, Barnes-lemma integrations, and summation of nested harmonic sums in HPLs. Completes the $1/\varepsilon$ and $\varepsilon^0$ parts missing from earlier work. Cross-checked against numerical alpha-parameter integration and Regge-limit expansion.
Records.location: Eq. (3box) propagators, Eq. (7MB) sevenfold MB representation, Eq. (3box-result) final analytic expression

Records.description: Three-loop massless planar triple-box topology with 10 propagators and 5 ISPs, 26 master integrals. Relevant for NNNLO QCD 2-jet production; first evaluated by Smirnov and later via DE by Henn, Mistlberger. Called TB in the paper. Canonical basis built with CANONICA in about two minutes; alphabet $\{x,1+x\}$ with $x=s/t$.

Records.authors: Henn, Johannes, Mistlberger, Bernhard, Smirnov, Vladimir A., Wasser, Pascal
Records.description: Three-loop planar ladder box (family A). Planar topology with three sequential boxes arranged in a ladder configuration.

Records.authors: Henn, Johannes M., Korchemsky, Gregory P., Mistlberger, Bernhard
Records.description: Three-loop four-point integral family with 15 denominator factors defined in Eq. (3.10) of Section 3.1, written in dual coordinates with $x_1=0$, $x_2=p_1$, $x_3=p_1+p_2$, $x_4=-p_4$ (external) and $x_6=k_1$, $x_7=k_2$, $x_8=k_3$ (loop momenta). All 4 external momenta are light-like ($p_i^2=0$) with momentum conservation. These integrals contribute to the Wilson loop with Lagrangian insertion $F(x)$ and ultimately to the four-loop cusp anomalous dimension via the Lagrangian-insertion method. The triple-ladder is the all-ones top sector (Fig. 3a). IBP reduction with FIRE5 yields 257 master integrals. A canonical-form differential equation basis is constructed using improved Baikov / leading-singularity techniques (Section 3.2) and integrated through transcendental weight 6 (finite order in $\varepsilon$ for finite quantities) in terms of HPLs over the alphabet $\{x, 1+x, (1+i\sqrt{x})/(1-i\sqrt{x})\}$ with $x=s/t$. Boundary conditions are fixed by regularity at $u=0$ ($x=-1$).
Records.familyName: G (three-loop four-point planar supergraph in dual coordinates)

Records.description: The massless on-shell triple box integral is computed using its Mellin-Barnes representation. The result is given in terms of harmonic polylogarithms.

Massless on-shell planar 3-loop 4-point triple box TB(s,t), 10 propagators, all external legs on-shell (p_i^2 = 0). Numerical evaluation at the Euclidean point (s,t) = (-1,-3) cross-checking the analytical result of Smirnov (hep-ph/0305142): TB(-1,-3) = Gamma(4+3*eps) [0.09874/eps^6 - 0.7669/eps^5 - 1.977/eps^4 - 0.7534/eps^3 - 4.747/eps^2 + 2.010/eps + 21.48], agreeing with the analytical answer at the 1% level. Full computation took several weeks on a 2 GHz Pentium IV, with object files ~500 MB. The propagator basis above is one standard routing for the triple box (k1,k2,k3 as loop momenta along the three boxes; the two interior 'rung' edges connect adjacent boxes); edge ordering matches a stacked-ladder vertex labelling V1..V8 with V5,V7 the upper-rung vertices and V6,V8 the lower-rung vertices.
Records.familyName: Massless on-shell planar triple box (10 propagators)
Records.location: Section 3.4, Fig. 6; numerical result for TB(-1,-3)

Names: G (three-loop four-point planar supergraph in dual coordinates)
